topic.what: Lightweight ANN for detecting CAN bus attacks in IoV
topic.how: Feed-forward ANN with layer neurons N_i=i×c; SCCE loss; RMSprop optimizer; processes normalized CAN ID and data bytes
topic.why_matters: Provides real-time intrusion detection for resource-constrained vehicular ECUs without encryption overhead

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of actual attacks correctly identified
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 99.84% DR of Song et al. 2020
characteristics.context: Average across CICIoV2024 (99.99%) and Car-Hacking (99.78%) datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of legitimate messages incorrectly flagged as attacks
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 0.05% FPR of Yang et al. 2021
characteristics.context: Average across both datasets; CICIoV2024 shows 0% FPR

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall correct classification rate across all attack types
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 99.92% of Song et al. 2020 on Car-Hacking

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to classify a single CAN message sample
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 0.15 ms of Song et al. 2020; 6 ms of Huang et al. 2024
characteristics.context: 5x faster than next-best CNN-based approach on Car-Hacking dataset

characteristics.what_it_measures: Memory footprint per sample during classification
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 2.2 MB of Song et al. 2020; 18.8 MB of Yang et al. 2021
characteristics.context: Suitable for embedded deployment on ARM Cortex and Raspberry Pi
10-fold cross-validation across attack types; demonstrates stable generalization

good_for: real-time CAN intrusion detection in vehicular ECUs
edge computing on ARM Cortex platforms
requires: numeric feature vectors (Arbitration ID + Data bytes)
novelty: Dynamic layer sizing formula N_i=i×c for lightweight ANN design
Sparse Categorical Cross-Entropy for memory-efficient multi-class training
RMSprop optimization for adaptive learning rate control
Interpretability via CAN message structure correlation with attack patterns
open_problems: Adversarial robustness evaluation using IBM ART toolbox
Distributed deployment using TinyML on vehicle networks
